title: utricularia tortilis var. andongensis
intro: Utricularia tortilis var. andongensis, also known as the Andong bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant species in the bladderwort family (Utriculariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Utricularia tortilis var. andongensis typically involve its roots, leaves, or entire plant being dried or processed into teas, infusions, or other medicinal products for consumption.
